title: Agentic Coding in Xcode: A First Step in Implementation (Part 1): From Concept to Runnable Environment
description: A hands-on roadmap based on Xcode 26.3 and Gemini CLI: You will go from 0 to 1 through environment setup, MCP connection and your first executable session.
content:
**TL;DR:** Apple is pushing AI-assisted development to new heights in Agentic Coding. This article is the first in a three-part series, "Agentic Coding on Xcode: A Guide to Deploying It," focusing on rapid deployment. It guides you step-by-step through getting the underlying MCP (Model Context Protocol) link between Xcode 26.3 and Gemini CLI (>0.27.3) running on your local machine, establishing the first step from "chat AI" to "executable intelligent agents." ## Foreword This is the first in a three-part series, inspired by Peter Friese's article (link at the end), but rewritten in Chinese with my own practical experience. The key point is: **You can run it on your local machine today.** Series Navigation: - Part 1 (This Article): From Concept to Environment Preparation - [Part 2: From Hints to a Runnable Demo (Newton’s Apple)](/posts/agentic-coding-xcode-gemini-02-hands-on/) - [Part 3: Common Pitfalls, Compatibility Issues, and Team Implementation Checklist](/posts/agentic-coding-xcode-gemini-03-troubleshooting/) ## Why Focus on Agentic Coding Now? Xcode 26.3 upgrades "AI-Assisted Coding" from chat to "Executable Agent Collaboration": - More than just suggestions, it allows modifications based on the project context. - It can trigger builds and iterate to fix errors. - It connects external agents with Xcode capabilities through MCP (Model Context Protocol). Simply put, it used to be "you write, AI reads," now it's more like "you set the goal, the agent runs the process." ## Prerequisites (Do not skip) To reproduce this process, the following conditions are recommended: 1.`Xcode 26.3` 2. `Gemini CLI >= 0.27.3`Why can't the Gemini version be too low? - In early Xcode 26.3 bridged returns,`structuredContent`There have been compatibility issues. - Subsequent patches for Gemini CLI have addressed this type of response (see references at the end of this article for the fix details). Install Gemini CLI: ```bash npm install -g @google/gemini-cli gemini --version ```## Open the MCP entry point in Xcode Path (macOS): 1. Open Xcode 2.`Xcode > Settings...`(shortcut key `⌘,`3. Enter`Intelligence`4. In`Model Context Protocol`Area Open`Xcode Tools` ![Enable Xcode Tools](https://peterfriese.dev/_astro/enable-xcode-tools.BjIu-Z5y_2LRtl.webp) _Image: Enable MCP support in Xcode Settings > Intelligence (Source: peterfriese.dev)_ This step is crucial: without enabling it, even if the CLI is configured correctly, you won't see Xcode's tool capabilities. ## Connect the Gemini CLI to the Xcode MCP Bridge Navigate to your project root directory and execute: ```bash gemini mcp add xcode-tools xcrun mcpbridge ```This will generate (or update) configuration files within the project: -`.gemini/settings.json`Recommended configuration: "Configure by project," not global: - Enable only in repositories that require Xcode capabilities - Avoid authorization confirmation prompts every time the CLI is opened - Cleaner switching between multiple projects, with clearer permission boundaries ## Starting a minimum viable session Follow this order: 1. Open the Xcode project first 2. Then execute the command in the project root directory`gemini`3. Accept Xcode's external tool authorization pop-up ![Allow Gemini CLI access](https://peterfriese.dev/_astro/allow-gemini-cli-access.BFKbhU-T_1wjQe6.webp) _Image: Xcode will pop up a permission confirmation pop-up upon initial connection (Source: peterfriese.dev)_ Within the CLI, you can first check if MCP is working correctly: ```text /mcp list /mcp desc ``` ![List available tools](https://peterfriese.dev/_astro/list-available-tools.B9ukbUdM_Z1OL9y8.webp) _Image: After a successful connection, the CLI should be able to list xcode-tools (Source: peterfriese.dev)_ If you can see`xcode-tools`The tool descriptions and instructions indicate that the connection has been established. ## The Mental Model You Should Develop The essence of this connection is: ```mermaid graph LR A[Gemini CLI] <--"MCP Protocol
(JSON-RPC)"--> B(xcrun mcpbridge) B <--"Xcode Internal API"--> C[Xcode Tools] style A fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b,stroke-width:2px style C fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#4a148c,stroke-width:2px style B fill:#fff,stroke:#666,stroke-dasharray: 5 5 ```In this role: - Gemini is responsible for understanding the goal and planning the steps. - Xcode provides engineering-level action capabilities (build, context, diagnostics). - You are responsible for final judgment and acceptance (Human in the loop). ## Summary You have now completed three of the most important things: - Confirmed version prerequisites. - Enabled MCP capabilities in Xcode. - Successfully connected and verified tool visibility using the Gemini CLI. In the next article, we'll jump straight into practical application: using a high-quality goal hint, we'll have the Agent collaboratively generate a "playable" SwiftUI + SpriteKit project, and explain how to let it fix compilation errors itself. ## References - Peter Friese: Agentic Coding in Xcode with Gemini CLI https://peterfriese.dev/blog/2026/agentic-coding-xcode-geminicli/ - Apple: Xcode 26.3 unlocks the power of agentic coding https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/02/xcode-26-point-3-unlocks-the-power-of-agentic-coding/ - Apple Docs: Setting up coding intelligence https://developer.apple.com/documentation/Xcode/setting-up-coding-intelligence - Apple Docs: Giving external agentic coding tools access to Xcode https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/giving-agentic-coding-tools-access-to-xcode - Gemini CLI release`v0.27.3` https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/releases/tag/v0.27.3
